Therapist: It sounds like the company and meaningful conversations with your friends were a big part of what made that night special. What do you think would happen to those connections if you didn't smoke with them anymore?

Patient: I don't know if it would be a bad thing... maybe it would make us appreciate each other more, you know? We'd find new ways to connect, new things to talk about. It might even make our friendships stronger.
Current patient message:

I don't know if it would be a bad thing... maybe it would make us appreciate each other more, you know? We'd find new ways to connect, new things to talk about. It might even make our friendships stronger.

Therapist:
step7_response: It sounds like you're feeling hopeful that quitting smoking could actually bring you closer to your friends. Have you ever noticed any specific moments or situations when you feel left out or like you don't fit in with your friends because of your smoking?

turns.user_message: Actually, yeah... I remember this one time we were all going to a wedding and they were all playing cricket in the garden. I wanted to play, but I couldn't because I was smoking and needed a break, then I got stuck smoking with some other people and missed the whole game. It felt like I was missing out, like the others were having more fun without me. Maybe quitting would help me be more present, you know?

step7_response: It sounds like feeling like you're missing out on social activities because of your smoking is a big concern for you. What if you could find ways to participate in those activities without needing a cigarette break, would that change how you feel about joining in?
